summary: Kept #65184 as the live canonical issue for the `Unit file openclaw-gateway.service does not exist` gateway-install subfamily, emitted a narrow `fix_needed` + `build_fix_artifact` plan instead of any close/merge actions, split the other live items into related or independent follow-ups, and routed linked PR #52293 to central OpenClaw security triage.

actions.reason: #65184 remains the best live canonical issue for the exact gateway-install failure cluster, and the artifact does not prove a landed or merge-ready fix path yet.
A narrow new-fix plan is needed before any further duplicate closeout because no viable canonical PR is proven for the live #65184 regression.
#42051 is useful related contributor work for an older sudo/home mismatch, but unresolved review findings and failing CI block treating it as the canonical fix for the live #65184 issue.
This is a related Linux/systemd install failure, but not the same root-cause path as the canonical migrated-root unit-file-missing issue.
#44417 belongs to the same gateway/systemd family, but it is a different env-detection failure and should stay open separately.
The report is in-family but mixes several different root-cause theories, so it should remain open as related rather than be auto-closed.
#63561 is related Linux/systemd fallout, but it is a distinct false-negative/status path and should remain open independently.
#68287 is a distinct node-service bug, not a duplicate of the gateway-install canonical issue.
Security-sensitive linked PR #52293 must be routed to central OpenClaw security triage; no ProjectClownfish mutation is allowed on that item.

needs_human: Route linked PR #52293 to central OpenClaw security triage; the preflight artifact marks it security-sensitive and ProjectClownfish must not mutate or merge it.
